title: Wilderness Emergency Medicine & TCCC Protocols
subject: Trauma Medicine & Field First Aid
description: Master MARCH PAWS trauma protocols, arterial tourniquet application, chest seals for tension pneumothorax, and wound packing.
learningObjectives: Apply MARCH PAWS protocol (Massive Bleeding, Airway, Respiration, Circulation, Hypothermia).
Apply high and tight CAT tourniquet for arterial extremity hemorrhage.
Pack junctional wounds using hemostatic gauze.

lessons.title: The MARCH PAWS Algorithm
lessons.content: In severe trauma, hemorrhage is the #1 preventable cause of death. MARCH stands for: M = Massive Bleeding (Tourniquets/Hemostatic Gauze), A = Airway (Recovery position / NPA), R = Respiration (Vented chest seals), C = Circulation (Pelvic binder / IV-IO), H = Hypothermia Prevention (Warm blankets).

lessons.title: Tourniquet Placement & Application
lessons.content: Place windlass tourniquets 2-3 inches above the wound (or high and tight over clothing during active danger). Tighten windlass until arterial bleeding stops completely and distal pulse is absent. Record application time on the TQ strap!

flashcards.definition: Tactical combat casualty care assessment priority: Massive Bleeding, Airway, Respiration, Circulation, Hypothermia.
Trapped air in the pleural cavity compressing the lung and heart, treated with vented chest seals or needle decompression.
Gauze impregnated with kaolin or chitosan that rapidly accelerates blood clotting.

questions.question: What is the absolute first priority in the MARCH trauma protocol?
Where should a tourniquet be placed relative to an arterial extremity bleed?
options: 2-3 inches above the wound (high and tight)

practicalChallenge.title: Self-Application Tourniquet Drill
practicalChallenge.instruction: Practice applying a CAT or SOFTT tourniquet to your non-dominant arm using one hand. Target completion time: under 30 seconds!
